title: oxalis ciliaris var. pageae
intro:
Oxalis ciliaris var. pageae, also known as the 'Page's wood sorrel', is a rare, perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and distinctive leaf structure.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and digestive issues, due to its high concentration of oxalic acid.

The health benefits of Oxalis ciliaris var. pageae are attributed to its diuretic and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve symptoms of kidney problems, reduce inflammation, and promote overall urinary health.

In herbal preparations, Oxalis ciliaris var. pageae is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in some traditional remedies for its potential to treat conditions such as cystitis and urinary tract infections.
